title: Iterators 1
description: Iterators provide a way to process sequences of elements. The `next()` method advances the iterator.
instructions: Complete the function using an iterator to produce the expected results.
hints: The `next()` method returns an `Option<&T>` - Some with the value, or None when done.
For a Vec<&str>, iter() returns items of type &&str, wrapped in Some.
The first call returns Some(&"banana"), the second Some(&"custard apple"), etc.
explanation: Iterators in Rust implement the `Iterator` trait with a `next()` method. Each call to `next()` returns `Some(value)` or `None` when exhausted. The `.iter()` method creates an iterator that borrows each element, so you get references. The iterator must be `mut` because `next()` changes its internal state.
